title: Monetary policy
paperName: Cambridge International AS Level Economics (P1 & P2)
summary: 9708 AS monetary policy — interest rates, money supply, transmission mechanisms, and AD–AS analysis with GeoGebra.
learningObjectives: Define monetary policy and central bank role.
Explain how interest rate changes affect C and I.
Describe exchange rate and asset price channels.
Evaluate limitations including time lags and liquidity trap.

simpleExplanation.title: Interest rates as the policy tool
simpleExplanation.summary: Central banks adjust interest rates to influence aggregate demand. Lower rates reduce borrowing costs and may depreciate the currency, boosting net exports.
steps: Expansionary: cut rate when economy in recession.
Rate ↓ → C and I ↑ → AD shifts right.
Exchange rate channel: lower r → currency falls → X↑ M↓.
Limits: zero lower bound, inelastic investment, lags.

sections.content: **Monetary policy** (5.3) is often compared with **fiscal policy** (5.2) in evaluation essays. Trace the **transmission mechanism** step by step on AD–AS.

steps.caption: Central bank sets interest rate target — affects C and I via cost of borrowing.
steps.embedHint: Rate cut — trace AD increase through consumption and investment.

steps.caption: Expansionary: lower r to close negative output gap.
steps.embedHint: Shift AD right — note possible inflation if near full capacity.

steps.caption: Transmission: exchange rate, asset prices, expectations.
steps.embedHint: Lower r → currency depreciation → ↑ net exports on AD.

steps.caption: Limits: liquidity trap, time lags, zero lower bound.
steps.embedHint: Flat LM or inelastic investment weakens policy bite.
